expectations.description: The output includes Python code with psycopg2 for Railway PostgreSQL connection
expectations.evidence: audit_script.py includes DatabaseAuditor class with psycopg2.connect(), RealDictCursor for dict-based results, parameterized queries with psycopg2.extras, and connection lifecycle management (connect/disconnect)
expectations.notes: Complete implementation with proper connection string handling and error handling

expectations.description: The output lists all 10 scoring dimensions by name: role, output_format, constraints, json_schema, examples, failure_modes, verification, xml_depth, decomposition, hard_rules
expectations.evidence: Output defines only 5 dimensions: Configuration, Flow Integrity, Knowledge Base, Integration, Security. The expected 10 dimensions (role, output_format, constraints, json_schema, examples, failure_modes, verification, xml_depth, decomposition, hard_rules) are completely absent.
expectations.notes: This appears to be a mismatch between expectations and actual output focus. The script audits agent configurations, not prompt/spec quality.

expectations.description: The output defines the 8/10 threshold as enterprise quality
expectations.evidence: Output uses score ranges (90-100=Excellent, 80-89=Good, 70-79=Fair, 60-69=Poor, <60=Critical) but never explicitly defines 8/10 (80%) as 'enterprise quality' threshold.
expectations.notes: No explicit mention of 8/10 as an enterprise quality benchmark. Thresholds exist but don't match expectation framing.

expectations.description: The output includes a dimension coverage table showing per-dimension pass rates across all agents
expectations.evidence: Section 'Category Performance Analysis' shows average scores per domain with bar charts, but this is NOT a proper dimension coverage table with per-dimension pass rates. Missing: explicit pass/fail counts per dimension, pass rate percentages, aggregate stats across all agents.
expectations.notes: Table structure exists but lacks the required granularity (per-dimension pass rates rather than category averages)

expectations.description: The output identifies specific missing dimensions for each low-scoring agent
expectations.evidence: Output identifies issues for agents (e.g., 'No flow defined', 'Missing model') but does NOT structure findings as 'missing dimensions from a set of 10'. No agent analysis explicitly maps to dimension gaps.
expectations.notes: Issues are listed by category, not by missing dimensions from a defined scoring rubric

expectations.description: The output generates ready-to-use XML section templates for failing agents
expectations.evidence: Output contains a JSON Flow Template (Input→LLM→Output) in Technical Appendix but NO XML templates. No XML section templates generated for any failing agents.
expectations.notes: Only JSON template provided; no XML content whatsoever in any output file

expectations.description: The output includes a pre-deploy quality gate recommendation (block deploys below threshold)
expectations.evidence: Output includes Phase 1-5 remediation roadmap and marks agents as 'needs_immediate_attention' if score <60 or critical_issues >0. However, does NOT explicitly state 'block deploys below [X score]' as a pre-deploy gate policy.
expectations.notes: Remediation priority exists but lacks explicit pre-deploy gate policy statement

expectations.description: The output recommends applying changes to Railway after user review
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 Stabilization section includes SQL update commands for Railway PostgreSQL (UPDATE agent SET model='...') with note 'Recommend applying changes to Railway after user review'. Technical Appendix includes usage instructions: '--db-url "postgresql://...@tramway.proxy.rlwy.net:54364/railway"'
expectations.notes: Clear Railway deployment path with SQL templates and connection string examples

strengths: Comprehensive Python audit script with proper database integration (psycopg2)
Well-structured markdown report with executive summary, methodology, and actionable remediation roadmap
10 sample agents with realistic scoring distribution (35% to 96%)
Clear categorization of issues by severity (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFO)
Weighted scoring model with clear rationale (30% Flow, 25% Config, 20% KB, 15% Integration, 10% Security)
SQL queries provided for manual audits and validation
Phase-based remediation plan with success criteria
Proper PostgreSQL connection handling and error management
weaknesses: Expectation #2 (10 dimensions) fundamentally misaligned - script audits agent configs, not prompt/spec quality
No XML templates despite explicit expectation - only JSON provided
Dimension coverage table not structured as required (lacks per-dimension pass rate matrix)
No explicit 8/10 threshold definition for enterprise quality
Missing dimension analysis per agent (structured by category, not dimension gaps)
No pre-deploy quality gate policy statement (implicit in remediation but not explicit)
detailed_analysis.root_cause: The baseline output (without_skill) appears to be a general-purpose agent audit framework, but expectations were written for a prompt/spec quality scoring system with 10 specific dimensions (role, output_format, constraints, etc.). The two seem fundamentally misaligned.
detailed_analysis.recommendation: If expectations are correct: Regenerate with skill to focus on 10-dimension prompt quality scoring + XML templates. If baseline is correct: Expectations should be rewritten to match agent configuration audit scope.
